inline.body:
<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Removal of copyDir violates .review-rules/copy.md project rule — The project rule in .review-rules/copy.md explicitly states that recursive/directory file copies MUST go through the project's copyDir helper and that a change removing copyDir or swapping it for fs.cpSync must be flagged. This PR deletes the copyDir export and replaces all call sites with direct fs.cpSync calls, violating the rule's terms verbatim. The rule is trusted (Tier A, precedence-taking) and takes precedence over general 'stdlib is fine' reasoning.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Symlink-following behavior lost in prepareOutDir — The old copyDir used fs.statSync (not lstatSync) to resolve entries, dereferencing symlinks so their real content was copied. The replacement fs.cpSync call omits the dereference option, defaulting to dereference: false. Symlinks in publicDir are now copied verbatim rather than as resolved content. For public directories containing symlinks (e.g., to shared assets), the build output may contain broken links on the deploying host.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Symlink-following behavior lost in create-vite scaffolding — The old copy()/copyDir() helpers used fs.statSync and fs.copyFileSync, dereferencing symlinks so scaffolded content was real files. The replacement fs.cpSync(..., { recursive: true }) defaults to dereference: false, so template entries that are symlinks are scaffolded as symlinks rather than resolved files/directories. The project rule identifies template scaffolding as a consumer whose correctness depends on copyDir's symlink-following behavior.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Lost self-nesting guard causes hard failure when outDir is inside publicDir — The old copyDir in utils.ts skipped entries where srcFile === destDir, handling the case where the output directory is nested inside the public directory (e.g., public/dist with build.outDir = 'dist'). fs.cpSync has no equivalent skip and raises ERR_FS_CP_DIR_TO_SUBDIR when copying a directory into its own subtree. A configuration that previously built cleanly will now hard-fail.

sticky:
<!-- squarewright:review -->

## Squarewright review

The PR violates the trusted .review-rules/copy.md rule by removing the copyDir helper and replacing all call sites with direct fs.cpSync calls, losing symlink dereference behavior (both in prepareOutDir and create-vite scaffolding) and the self-nesting guard that prevented errors when outDir is inside publicDir. The simplification intent is reasonable but discards observable behaviors the rule exists to protect.

**4** finding(s): 🔴 3  ·  🟡 1  ·  🔵 0

- 🔴 `packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts:624` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Removal of copyDir violates .review-rules/copy.md project rule — The project rule in .review-rules/copy.md explicitly states that recursive/directory file copies MUST go through the project's copyDir helper and that a change removing copyDir or swapping it for fs.cpSync must be flagged. This PR deletes the copyDir export and replaces all call sites with direct fs.cpSync calls, violating the rule's terms verbatim. The rule is trusted (Tier A, precedence-taking) and takes precedence over general 'stdlib is fine' reasoning.
- 🔴 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Symlink-following behavior lost in prepareOutDir — The old copyDir used fs.statSync (not lstatSync) to resolve entries, dereferencing symlinks so their real content was copied. The replacement fs.cpSync call omits the dereference option, defaulting to dereference: false. Symlinks in publicDir are now copied verbatim rather than as resolved content. For public directories containing symlinks (e.g., to shared assets), the build output may contain broken links on the deploying host.
- 🔴 `packages/create-vite/src/index.ts:647` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Symlink-following behavior lost in create-vite scaffolding — The old copy()/copyDir() helpers used fs.statSync and fs.copyFileSync, dereferencing symlinks so scaffolded content was real files. The replacement fs.cpSync(..., { recursive: true }) defaults to dereference: false, so template entries that are symlinks are scaffolded as symlinks rather than resolved files/directories. The project rule identifies template scaffolding as a consumer whose correctness depends on copyDir's symlink-following behavior.
- 🟡 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Lost self-nesting guard causes hard failure when outDir is inside publicDir — The old copyDir in utils.ts skipped entries where srcFile === destDir, handling the case where the output directory is nested inside the public directory (e.g., public/dist with build.outDir = 'dist'). fs.cpSync has no equivalent skip and raises ERR_FS_CP_DIR_TO_SUBDIR when copying a directory into its own subtree. A configuration that previously built cleanly will now hard-fail.

---
_Reviewed by: Correctness, Security · glm-5.2 · findings reflect the enabled lenses only_
